battles_present: Moore's Creek Bridge, Feb. 27, 1776 — nominal commanding officer; ill and unable to command at battle; captured next day in his tent

Moore's Creek Bridge, Feb. 27, 1776 — effective commanding officer; led charge across bridge; killed in action. Sources disagree on wound count: Moore AAR says 'upwards of twenty balls'; NCpedia says 9 bullets and 24 swan shot; Wikipedia says 20 musket balls.
Moore's Creek Bridge — recruiter and officer; led advance company of swordsmen; wounded and captured
Moore's Creek Bridge, Feb. 27, 1776 — led advance company of Highland broadswordsmen across bridge; killed at or near east bank
Moore's Creek Bridge, Feb. 27, 1776 — Patriot field commander; occupied west bank camp, conducted bridge deception withdrawal, held east bank earthworks
Moore's Creek Bridge, Feb. 27, 1776 — constructed and held east bank earthworks; NPS archaeology confirms earthworks exactly on original position
Moore's Creek Bridge, Feb. 27, 1776 — commanded New Hanover Volunteer Company of Rangers; flanking position east bank with Lillington
Moore's Creek Bridge — deserted Loyalist camp before battle to warn Patriots of dawn attack; intelligence function individual
